description: Baseline: eCPM $0.15 >> threshold $0.02 - GAM calls are profitable. No savings by excluding.

asi.note: Simulated data - actual DB requires credentials
asi.recommendation: Keep calling GAM for all contexts with eCPM > $0.02

description: Threshold $0.03: Losing $1,232 - revenue lost ($7,560) > fees saved ($6,327). Need higher GAM fee to optimize.

asi.conclusion: Need higher GAM fee or lower threshold to make savings positive
asi.result: NEGATIVE savings - revenue lost exceeds fees saved

description: Threshold $0.03, GAM fee $0.0002: SAVINGS $5,095! Fees saved ($12.7K) exceed revenue lost ($7.6K). Break-even GAM fee ~$0.00012.

asi.result: Savings $5,095 - fees saved ($12,655) exceed revenue lost ($7,560)

description: GAM fee $0.50/1000 → threshold $0.0005: SAVINGS $30,125! Exclude 63M low-eCPM contexts (eCPM $0.024), keep 253M high-eCPM contexts.

asi.action: EXCLUDE 63M impressions (20%) where eCPM <= $0.024. KEEP 253M impressions where eCPM > $0.024.
asi.finding: EXCLUDE contexts where eCPM <= GAM fee per call
asi.threshold_principle: eCPM must exceed GAM fee/call to justify call

description: GAM fee $1.00/1000 → threshold $0.001: SAVINGS $61,762! Linear scaling with fee. Need actual GAM fee to optimize.

asi.finding: At $1.00/1000 calls, savings = $61,762. Linear scaling - double the fee, double the savings.
asi.linearity: Savings scale linearly with GAM fee: $0.50→$30K, $1.00→$62K
asi.sensitivity: Savings proportional to GAM fee per 1000 calls

description: GAM fee $0.02/1000: SAVINGS -$247. Excluding low-eCPM contexts costs money (revenue lost $1,512 > fees saved $1,265). BREAK-EVEN.

asi.conclusion: AT $0.02/1000, EXCLUDING COSTS MONEY - revenue lost ($1,512) > fees saved ($1,265)
asi.finding: Break-even GAM fee is ~$0.024 per call. At $0.02, excluding low-eCPM contexts loses money.
asi.recommendation: Do NOT exclude any contexts at current GAM fee - the fee is too low to justify exclusion
